jokes.text: People from high school only friend you, like, after business hours if they wanna hook up.
jokes.setup: Discussion about high school friend request
jokes.punchline: Cynical but accurate observation about social media timing
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor that feels authentic to the character and relatable to the audience. Good economy and specificity.

jokes.text: Did Amelia Earhart wait to be asked to fly around the world? Definitely not. She asked. And then they said no. But she still did it. And she died, but she, like, died doing it.
jokes.setup: Empowering reference to Amelia Earhart as motivation
jokes.punchline: Escalates to acknowledge she died, undermining the inspirational message
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of motivational speak. Takes expected inspirational reference and adds dark reality for comic effect.

jokes.text: Troy Megliarino. He spent a hot sec in jail, but he lives in South Jersey and I know he'd steal a car and come up for the night.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of criminal behavior as a positive dating quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ilana's casual attitude toward red flags. Good economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: You know, I'm picking all white dudes right now. I must be craving pink dick.
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude but matter-of-fact analysis
jokes.explanation: Very character-specific humor. Ilana's blunt sexual commentary delivered with casual confidence.

jokes.text: Henry Rowdenbush, had a unibrow all through middle school and then he started waxing it and all of a sudden it was like, glasses off, you're fucking gorgeous. It's like 'She's All That.'
jokes.punchline: References makeover movie trope but gender-flipped
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference with gender reversal. Specific details about the unibrow make it more vivid.

jokes.text: I feel like I'm on coke right now.
jokes.setup: Excited energy from listing potential hookups
jokes.punchline: Compares dating enthusiasm to drug high
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ilana's intensity, though the comparison is fairly standard.

jokes.text: We are, like, feminist heroes right now.
jokes.setup: After aggressive pursuit of multiple men
jokes.punchline: Claims feminist status for what's essentially thirst
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-driven irony. Ilana's ability to reframe behavior as empowering is perfectly captured.

jokes.text: So, okay, that's 36 guys that we've been rejected by and one lady.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact accounting of their failure rate
jokes.explanation: Good deflating moment after their confidence. Abbi's practical nature coming through.

jokes.text: The Internet is so '90s. Let's go find some guys IRL. / You know, you can just say 'in real life.' It's the same number of syllables.
jokes.setup: Ilana using internet abbreviation in speech
jokes.punchline: Abbi pointing out the logical flaw
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke about internet culture. Good character dynamic with Abbi as the practical one.

jokes.text: When I was five, I was actually an extra in the movie 'Philadelphia.' It filmed right near my dad's office. So I learned about AIDS before a lot of my friends.
jokes.setup: Casual mention of being in a movie as a child
jokes.punchline: Reveals she learned about AIDS early due to the film's subject matter
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with innocent delivery. Unexpected connection between childhood movie experience and heavy subject matter.

jokes.text: Cell phones are so close to being perfect, except that I just miss that there's like a-- Dial tone. Dial tone. Yes, are you kidding me? No, yeah. You're trying to make a connection with someone and there's no sound anymore. It's just like, hi, you're there.
jokes.punchline: Nostalgia for dial tones as meaningful connection
jokes.explanation: Meandering observational humor that feels more like rambling. Character-appropriate but not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Freeze, ass bitch, don't move, don't breathe. When was the last time you were tested? Uh, last week? Oh, my God, great. Okay, she's gonna want to shower first, probably, and you know, just be patient, okay? Okay.
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate sexual coaching in public
jokes.explanation: Peak Ilana behavior - no boundaries, oversharing, aggressive wingwoman tactics. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: You should have a sex with him. Tonight.
jokes.punchline: Awkward phrasing 'have a sex' instead of 'have sex'
jokes.explanation: Minor language joke that fits character's sometimes awkward phrasing. Small but authentic detail.

jokes.text: On the-- on the pink? Bitch, please. I haven't even started yet.
jokes.setup: Abbi asking if she's giving up on guys
jokes.punchline: Ilana's confidence about her dating prospects
jokes.explanation: Callback to 'pink dick' comment with typical Ilana bravado. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: You're not one of those hot girls that says she's gonna text and then doesn't, right?
jokes.setup: Guy trying to secure future contact
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing his dating insecurities
jokes.explanation: Good insight into dating anxiety. The neediness undermines his attempt to be cool.

jokes.text: I'm the hot girl at this bar. Me, me is. Yes-- calling my mom.
jokes.setup: Abbi feeling confident about being called hot
jokes.punchline: Immediately calls her mom to share the news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abbi moment - rare confidence immediately deflated by childlike impulse to tell mom. Great character writing.

jokes.text: Yeah, but his abuela's in the hospital, which sucks, but 'Silver Linings Playbook,' we have the place to ourselves.
jokes.punchline: Finding the bright side of someone's hospitalized grandmother
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Ilana humor - acknowledging something is bad while immediately pivoting to personal benefit. Great structure.

jokes.text: Dayum! That penis is pink.
jokes.setup: First sight of her hookup naked
jokes.punchline: Confirms her earlier 'pink dick' prediction
jokes.explanation: Direct callback to earlier 'craving pink dick' line. Simple but effective payoff.

jokes.text: There's something I should tell you. I'm bisexual.
jokes.punchline: Revelation that doesn't actually change anything for a straight woman
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up expectation of problematic revelation, delivers harmless information instead.

jokes.text: That is true masculinity. You are truly evolved and I am truly wet.
jokes.punchline: Ilana's over-the-top positive response ending crudely
jokes.explanation: Classic Ilana - takes progressive stance to its crude logical conclusion. Good escalation from philosophical to physical.

jokes.text: Has anyone seen a phone? Is there a phone? / I lose my phone all the time, too. I mean, I don't, like, lose it, lose it, I just, like, I see it fall into the toilet, but...
jokes.punchline: Woman's rambling non-helpful response about toilet incidents
jokes.explanation: Minor supporting character moment. Realistic rambling response but not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Okay, you guys, listen. I lost my phone. This guy is supposed to text me. He called me hot. So I'm cute, but you guys get it. Can you guys help me find it? / Yes. / I'm gonna need you to link arms. We're gonna sweep the bar.
jokes.setup: Abbi asking for help finding phone
jokes.punchline: Organizing strangers into an elaborate search formation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of desperate behavior. Abbi's organizational instincts applied to ridiculous situation.

jokes.text: Mind if we take a break and I go down on you for 45 minutes?
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific time commitment for oral sex
jokes.explanation: The specificity of '45 minutes' makes this funnier than generic dirty talk. Good precision comedy.

jokes.text: Don't ever let society change you.
jokes.punchline: Treating oral sex offer as profound character trait
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ilana response - finds deep meaning in sexual encounter. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: We've all moved on.
jokes.setup: Abbi continuing to search for phone with music louder
jokes.punchline: Group's collective dismissal of her quest
jokes.explanation: Good deflation moment. Clean cutoff of Abbi's increasingly desperate search.

jokes.text: Dude, I'm gonna be so pissed if you're dead, you've got to call me back. I'm freaking out.
jokes.punchline: Being angry at potential corpse for not calling back
jokes.explanation: Great character logic - Ilana's self-centered concern even when worried about friend. Good absurdist reasoning.

jokes.text: She doesn't have, like, street smarts like me, you know, and no upper body strength.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she's worried about Ilana
jokes.punchline: Adding physical weakness as additional concern
jokes.explanation: Good character reversal - usually Abbi is the more vulnerable one. The physical detail adds specificity.

jokes.text: Lincoln, just get a dog. / Ilana, I can't inflict upon a dog the crazy life of a dentist.
jokes.setup: Ilana suggesting Lincoln get a dog
jokes.punchline: Lincoln viewing dentistry as traumatic for pets
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic. Lincoln's neurotic self-awareness about his profession affecting pet ownership.

jokes.text: She didn't even say 'bye, Lincoln.' That takes one more second. People have no phone etiquette these days.
jokes.setup: Lincoln complaining about Ilana hanging up
jokes.punchline: Philosophical complaint about modern manners
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lincoln's politeness obsession. Relatable observational humor.

jokes.text: I thought you got SVU'd.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she was worried about Abbi
jokes.punchline: Using TV crime show as verb for being murdered
jokes.explanation: Brilliant neologism. Perfect millennial reference turning pop culture into casual verb. Very quotable.

jokes.text: My son-in-law's such a disgrace. He went to Cornell. / I'm fine with the tsunami, but not in St. Bart's. / It's the tenth horse that died this year. That's ten horses I've had to replace.
jokes.punchline: Absurd first-world problems and privilege
jokes.explanation: Good social satire. Specific absurd details make it more than generic rich people jokes.

jokes.text: Why does anybody come up here? It is a horrible, vapid wasteland.
jokes.punchline: Extreme disgust at wealthy neighborhood
jokes.explanation: Standard class-based humor but delivered with character-appropriate intensity.

jokes.text: Abbi, you should really wear a ball gown IRL.
jokes.setup: Seeing Abbi in formal dress at Met
jokes.punchline: Suggesting formal wear for everyday life
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier 'IRL' usage. Visual comedy element with costume contrast.

jokes.text: I had this-- this-- I had one. This is mine, though. This is the mine, this is easy to--
jokes.punchline: Confused rambling about ownership
jokes.explanation: Minor cringe moment with elderly confusion. More awkward than funny.

jokes.text: Ilana. / Are you kidding me? Ilana, stop. / No, this park is full of pervs. Get ready, because everybody's gonna whip their phone out and we're gonna find yours in three, two, one.
jokes.setup: Ilana has an idea to find the phone
jokes.punchline: Ilana strips to make people take photos
jokes.explanation: Peak Ilana behavior - extreme solution to simple problem. Physical comedy with character-driven logic.

jokes.text: Only in NYC! If I were you, I'd pull my phone out and take a pic!
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on her own spectacle
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor. Ilana both creating and commenting on the NYC experience.

jokes.text: Why is no one taking a picture of her? / That girl's been here for hours.
jokes.punchline: Reveal that public nudity is common here
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Ilana's dramatic gesture. Good New York local knowledge joke.

jokes.text: Hey, one of you stole our phone. We know you're in here!
jokes.punchline: Accusing ducks of theft
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperation. Abbi's organizational instincts applied to impossible situation.

jokes.text: We live in an ugly city of monsters.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic condemnation of all NYC
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of dramatic overreaction. Typical Ilana hyperbole.

jokes.text: Times Square, ew! We can't go in here, dude, it's-- it's too disgusting.
jokes.setup: Phone signal leading to Times Square
jokes.punchline: Extreme revulsion at tourist area
jokes.explanation: Standard NYC local vs. tourist humor. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: It's gonna be unfair to whoever I marry, because I'll probably, like, always compare them to Ben. It's gonna just tear us apart immediately, or throughout.
jokes.punchline: Already planning how one beer will ruin future marriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abbi catastrophizing. Takes minor encounter and projects it into lifelong consequences.

jokes.text: Fuck you both, you yuppie trash! Tax the rich!
jokes.punchline: Random political outburst at wrong targets
jokes.explanation: Good random NYC encounter. Misplaced class anger adds to Times Square chaos.

jokes.text: What's hotter than a pink dick with a sense of humor? I mean, a black dick, but...
jokes.punchline: Immediate racial comparison undermining the compliment
jokes.explanation: Callback to pink dick theme but ventures into uncomfortable racial territory. Less clever than crude.

jokes.text: You know that you're so anti-racist sometimes that you're actually really racist?
jokes.punchline: Calling out performative progressivism
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on liberal guilt and racial fetishization. Self-aware show criticism.

jokes.text: We are Statutory Crepe. Everything you're about to see will be fully improvised.
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun name combining 'statutory rape' with 'crepe'
jokes.explanation: Typical bad improv troupe name. Offensive but not particularly clever wordplay.

jokes.text: Mom. / Yes, son? / I need to talk to you about my penis. / Yes, son? I love answering questions about penises, even though I have a vagina! Ha ha ha!
jokes.setup: Improv scene starting with mother-son dynamic
jokes.punchline: Immediately goes to crude sexual territory with bad delivery
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad improv. Crude for shock value without wit. The cringe is the point.

jokes.text: I'm embarrassed that I'm here. I'm embarrassed that he was inside of me.
jokes.punchline: Regret extends from show to sexual encounter
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and character moment. Connects bad performance to intimate regret with great economy.

jokes.text: How much for this shirt? I can-- One million dollars.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly high price without setup
jokes.explanation: Weak improv - random = funny approach without clever structure

jokes.text: Oh, my God, that was the worst thing I've ever seen. I feel like I've been hate-crimed.
jokes.punchline: Treating bad comedy as violent crime
jokes.explanation: Excellent hyperbolic response. 'Hate-crimed' is a perfect verb choice for the experience.

jokes.text: I bet the freak was seeing a play and turned it off to be polite.
jokes.punchline: Assumes phone thief has better theater etiquette than them
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation. Criminal has better manners than the protagonists.

jokes.text: Times Square, Central Park? Upper East Side, means it's a tourist. Ew!
jokes.punchline: Deducing and being disgusted by tourist status
jokes.explanation: Good detective work with character-appropriate disgust. NYC local superiority complex.

jokes.text: The last place every tourist goes. / The airport? / No, not-- Not literally the last place. Magnolia Bakery.
jokes.setup: Abbi figuring out where thief went
jokes.punchline: Misdirection from literal to cultural 'last stop'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup and specific NYC tourist knowledge. Clean structure.

jokes.text: Oh, no, wait, that was, like, a pinto bean.
jokes.setup: Tourist finding things in her purse
jokes.punchline: Mistaking bean for candy
jokes.explanation: Minor absurd detail about tourist's confusion. Not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: I must have taken that on incident. I swear. Like, that makes sense 'cause I called my mom and she was like, way nicer than usual.
jokes.setup: Tourist explaining how she got Abbi's phone
jokes.punchline: Her mom was unusually nice because she called from wrong number
jokes.explanation: Good accidental revelation about family dynamics. Logical absurdity.

jokes.text: This purse is enormous. I carry it to make me look petite.
jokes.punchline: Using large accessory for opposite effect
jokes.explanation: Good fashion logic joke. Clean delivery and relatable vanity.

jokes.text: Can I get a one-word suggestion? / I heard 'cunnilingus.'
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual suggestion for family-friendly request
jokes.explanation: Typical bad improv shock humor. Crude without cleverness.

jokes.text: I've got a turtle head poking out!
jokes.punchline: Scatological reference disguised as innocent phrase
jokes.explanation: More intentionally bad improv. Bathroom humor without sophistication.

jokes.text: I am a robot and I'm programmed to be horny.
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'horny' (sexual/having horns)
jokes.explanation: Basic pun with sexual content. Intentionally hacky improv material.

jokes.text: I made a poopy in my dipey.
jokes.punchline: Baby talk about defecation
jokes.explanation: Bottom-tier improv humor. Juvenile scatological content without wit.

jokes.text: You are so gorgeous and sexually skilled it's scary, but your improv show was just not good.
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest feedback delivered kindly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abbi moment - honest to a fault. Great structure balancing compliment with devastating criticism.

jokes.text: That was one of our best shows. / Shut up, you're an idiot.
jokes.punchline: Abbi's immediate harsh response
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from polite to brutal. Shows Abbi's limits for BS when artistic integrity is at stake.

jokes.text: Thank you for your services. But seriously, thank you for your services, they were good.
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly formal thanks for sexual performance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Abbi - trying to be polite about sex like a business transaction

jokes.text: Don't put your shirt on before you leave. What's the point?
jokes.punchline: Abbi appreciating his body despite hating his art
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Abbi can separate physical from intellectual attraction. Practical honesty.

jokes.text: Just give me a second. Oh, yeah. Just look away, I just need-- I ate banana pudding, I just--
jokes.setup: Ben arriving for date with Abbi
jokes.punchline: Immediate digestive issues from dessert
jokes.explanation: Basic bodily function humor. Awkward timing but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I offer clients incentives to stay with the bank, so. / Cool. / You know, I used to have an office that didn't have a window. They just moved me into an office with a window. / Oh, that's great. I mean, it's much better to have a window. / Much better, I look out all the time.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to make conversation on date
jokes.punchline: Incredibly boring office details delivered with importance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. Captures boring office worker small talk perfectly.

jokes.text: That was like a year ago. / 13 months, 14, 13 months, 14 months.
jokes.setup: Ben trying to be precise about timeline
jokes.punchline: Obsessive correction that makes no practical difference
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Ben's tedious precision. Realistic awkward date behavior.

jokes.text: I was so worried, I baked a whole cake. And then I ate a whole cake.
jokes.setup: Lincoln explaining his worry about Abbi
jokes.punchline: Stress eating progression from baking to consuming
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lincoln behavior - anxious productivity followed by emotional eating. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: He told me the rules to some game, Settlers of Catan, for like, 40 minutes. / Oh, God, that should take, like, ten minutes, tops.
jokes.setup: Abbi describing boring date with Ben
jokes.punchline: Ilana's expert knowledge of board game explanation timing
jokes.explanation: Excellent nerd culture knowledge joke. Ilana's unexpected expertise makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, no, I still slept with him. / Oh! / Yeah! / Nice--
jokes.setup: After describing how boring Ben was
jokes.punchline: Reveals she had sex anyway
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of expectations. Perfect Abbi priorities - bored but horny. Good structure.

jokes.text: You know what's good for that? Is if you just get a napkin or a paper towel, or a thick napkin--
jokes.setup: Lincoln and Ilana making cake mess
jokes.punchline: Ben's overly detailed cleaning advice
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Ben's need to over-explain simple concepts. Realistic social awkwardness.

jokes.text: And learn Settlers of Catan. It's honestly not that hard.
jokes.punchline: Still pushing the board game despite the breakup
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and character moment. Ben's obliviousness to social cues. Good persistence gag.

jokes.text: Where is Central Park? / You're in it. / Oh, my God. Central Perk. / The one with Gunther. And 'Smelly Cat'?
jokes.punchline: Conflating Central Park with Friends coffee shop
jokes.explanation: Good tourist ignorance joke. Specific pop culture confusion makes it more than generic stupidity.

jokes.text: For dick pics and sex media in general. / Why would you need a phone from, like, 'The Wire' for that? / I'm still on my family plan and I don't fully understand the cloud and I don't trust it. I mean, I can't risk having just dicks floating around. I feel like they're gonna, like, pop up on my parents' phone one day.
jokes.punchline: Fear of family seeing explicit content due to cloud confusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant modern technology anxiety. 'Dicks floating around' is perfect phrase. Very quotable and relatable.

jokes.text: I can't risk having just dicks floating around. I feel like they're gonna, like, pop up on my parents' phone one day.
jokes.punchline: Vivid image of dick pics appearing on parents' devices
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy about technology anxiety and family embarrassment

jokes.text: How many dicks are floating around? / You're counting a lot. / Like, in the late 20s?
jokes.punchline: Ilana's impressive digital collection count
jokes.explanation: Good callback to 'floating around' with specific impressive number. Character-appropriate promiscuity.
